title: poa caespitosa var. tenera
intro: Poa caespitosa var. tenera, also known as the hairy meadow-grass or mountain bluegrass, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Poa caespitosa var. tenera can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and utilize its health benefits.
